name: Satay with Peanut Sauce
recipeIngredient: Beef (1 lb, thinly sliced into strips measuring ½ inch wide x 2 inches long), pork, or chicken
Grated fresh ginger root (2 tbsp, minced or pressed)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the marinade, combine brown sugar, curry powder, crunchy peanut butter, soy sauce, fresh lime juice, and garlic in a shallow dish.
2. Thread the meat pieces onto water-soaked bamboo skewers, weaving them lengthwise to create a serpentine design.
3. Place the skewers into the soy sauce mixture. Marinate at room temperature for at least 2 hours or refrigerate overnight for a more intense flavor.
4. For the peanut sauce, combine crunchy peanut butter, coconut milk, fresh lemon juice, soy sauce, brown sugar or molasses, grated ginger root, minced garlic, and ground cayenne pepper in a saucepan over moderate heat. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens to the consistency of heavy cream (about 15 minutes). Transfer to a food processor or blender and puree briefly. Add chicken stock and heavy cream; blend until smooth. Reserve.
5. Prepare a moderate charcoal fire for grilling or preheat the broiler.
6. Cook the skewered meat, turning several times and basting with the marinade, over medium-hot coals (or under the broiler) until crispy on the outside but still moist inside, about 6 to 8 minutes.
7. Sprinkle with lime zest and garnish with cilantro leaves. Serve with the peanut sauce for dipping. Makes about 18 skewers; serve as 2 per appetizer or 4 or more as a main course.
8. For a main dish, serve with steamed rice, salad, and grilled or steamed vegetables such as asparagus.
